name: Razorpay Payment — Event Sourcing
scenario: Razorpay needs a complete audit trail for all payment events (RBI compliance). Replace the current CRUD payment table with an event-sourced model where every state change is stored as an immutable event.

core.description: DomainEvent sealed interface with 4 records: PaymentInitiatedEvent, ProcessingStartedEvent, BankTimeoutEvent, PaymentFailedEvent. All immutable, with relevant fields.
PaymentAggregate: reconstruct(List<DomainEvent>) static factory replays events into state. Separate apply(XxxEvent) methods. getStatus(), getAmount() accessors.
EventStore @Service: append(aggregateId, event), loadEvents(aggregateId) → ordered List<StoredEvent>. Append-only (no update methods).
TemporalQuery: reconstructUpTo(events, N) → state at event N. Used for dispute resolution.
PaymentStatusProjection: rebuild from event store, getStatus(paymentId) → current status.
@SpringBootTest (4 tests): full lifecycle events → status FAILED, temporal query at event 1 → INITIATED, projection index correct, snapshot stores/loads correctly. All 4 pass.
README: explain why event sourcing for payments and not for shopping carts (audit + regulatory requirement).

bonus.description: JPA-backed EventStore: EventRecord entity with unique constraint on (aggregate_id, sequence). @DataJpaTest: append + load from H2.
Optimistic concurrency: append with expectedVersion parameter. ConcurrentModificationException if version mismatch. Test: wrong version throws.
EventUpcaster: PaymentFailedEvent V1 → V2 (add retryable field). Test: TIMEOUT → retryable=true, FRAUD → retryable=false.

ai_policy_explanation: 5 hints. Events are past tense: PaymentInitiatedEvent (not InitiatePaymentEvent). Reconstruct via fold: create empty aggregate, apply each event in order — each event mutates one or more fields. Sealed interface: compiler verifies all subtypes handled in switch. Append-only: EventStore has no update() or delete() methods — only append() and load(). Temporal query: stream().limit(N) — apply only first N events to get state at that point in time.
